fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and high analyst estimates are overshadowed by significant operating losses and a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY, with analyst estimates for current Q and FY showing substantial expected increases.
Forward P/E of 36.35 is lower than the trailing P/E of n/a, suggesting growth is expected to outpace earnings.
PEG ratio of 0.78 indicates potential undervaluation relative to expected earnings growth.
Strong current ratio of 3.83 and quick ratio of 2.85 suggest good short-term liquidity.
Total debt of $280.42M is manageable relative to total cash of $439.70M.
negatives: Operating margin of -8.6% and profit margin of -8.5% indicate the company is not profitable on an operating basis.
Return on equity of -6.1% and return on assets of -3.3% show poor returns on invested capital.
Free cash flow is negative at $-445.58M TTM, indicating cash burn.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 27.46 is very high, suggesting the stock is priced for substantial future growth.
Debt/Equity ratio of 25.36 is extremely high, indicating significant leverage.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention recently, with articles highlighting substantial transceiver orders and a rapidly shifting narrative. Despite a massive 6-month stock price increase and ongoing discussions about whether the stock is a 'bubble or bottleneck,' analysts are largely positive, with one article suggesting the story is "shifting with big transceiver orders and targets." The company's stock has surged over 600% in the last six months, and while some question its sustainability, others remain long, anticipating continued growth.
